 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e","title":"EPIC: CI/CD & Quality Gates","description":"# Epic: CI/CD & Quality Gates

## Overview
Set up comprehensive CI/CD pipeline to ensure quality and enable confident deployments.

## Background & Reasoning
A project of this scope needs automated quality gates:
- Catch bugs before they reach users
- Verify installer actually works on real Ubuntu
- Ensure website builds correctly
- Maintain code quality standards

## CI Components

### 1. Code Quality
- ESLint for TypeScript/JavaScript
- Prettier for formatting
- shellcheck for bash scripts
- TypeScript type checking

### 2. Website Tests
- Unit tests for components
- Build verification
- Lighthouse CI for performance

### 3. Installer Tests
- Spin Ubuntu VM (GitHub Actions runner or cloud)
- Run full install.sh
- Execute acfs doctor
- Verify all tools present

### 4. Integration Tests
- Manifest parsing
- Generator outputs
- Doctor check coverage

## GitHub Actions Workflows

```yaml
# .github/workflows/ci.yml
- lint-and-type-check
- test-website
- test-installer-ubuntu-25
- shellcheck-scripts
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All PRs require passing CI
- [ ] Installer tested on real Ubuntu
- [ ] Website build verified
- [ ] shellcheck passes on all .sh files
- [ ] Coverage reports generated
- [ ] Deployment preview for PRs

## Considerations
- Keep CI fast (< 10 min for most checks)
- Installer test can be slower (separate workflow)
- Use caching aggressively
- Matrix test multiple Ubuntu versions","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:22:08.720517-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:22:08.720517-05:00","labels":["ci","epic"]}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e.1","title":"Set up GitHub Actions for website CI","description":"# Task: Set up GitHub Actions for Website CI

## Description
Create GitHub Actions workflow for website quality gates.

## Workflow File
`.github/workflows/website.yml`

## Jobs

### 1. lint-and-typecheck
```yaml
- uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v1
- run: bun install
- run: bun run lint
- run: bun run type-check
```

### 2. build
```yaml
- uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v1
- run: bun install
- run: bun run build
```

### 3. test (if tests exist)
```yaml
- run: bun test
```

### 4. lighthouse (optional)
```yaml
- uses: treosh/lighthouse-ci-action@v10
  with:
    urls: |
      http://localhost:3000/
    uploadArtifacts: true
```

## Triggers
- Push to main
- Pull requests to main

## Caching
```yaml
- uses: actions/cache@v3
  with:
    path: ~/.bun/install/cache
    key: ${{ runner.os }}-bun-${{ hashFiles('**/bun.lock') }}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Runs on every PR
- [ ] Lint + typecheck required to pass
- [ ] Build verification
- [ ] Caching for speed

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e.2","title":"Set up GitHub Actions for installer testing","description":"# Task: Set up GitHub Actions for Installer Testing

## Description
Create GitHub Actions workflow that tests the installer on real Ubuntu.

## Workflow File
`.github/workflows/installer.yml`

## Jobs

### 1. shellcheck
Fast job that runs on every PR:
```yaml
- uses: ludeeus/action-shellcheck@master
  with:
    scandir: './scripts'
    additional_files: 'install.sh'
```

### 2. test-ubuntu-25
Full installer test (may be slow):
```yaml
runs-on: ubuntu-latest
container:
  image: ubuntu:25.04
steps:
  - uses: actions/checkout@v4
  - name: Run installer
    run: |
      bash install.sh --yes --mode vibe
  - name: Run doctor
    run: |
      su - ubuntu -c "acfs doctor"
  - name: Verify key tools
    run: |
      su - ubuntu -c "ntm --help"
      su - ubuntu -c "ubs --help"
      # ... etc
```

### 3. test-ubuntu-24
Same but on 24.04 LTS for compatibility.

## Triggers
- Push to main
- Pull requests touching install.sh or scripts/

## Matrix Testing
```yaml
strategy:
  matrix:
    ubuntu: ['24.04', '25.04']
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] shellcheck passes on all scripts
- [ ] Installer works on Ubuntu 24.04
- [ ] Installer works on Ubuntu 25.04
- [ ] All tools verified after install
- [ ] acfs doctor exits 0","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:21.687302-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:21.687302-05:00","labels":["ci","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-17e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:27:21.688994-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2bn","title":"EPIC: Onboarding TUI","description":"# Epic: Onboarding TUI

## Overview
Build the interactive `onboard` command that teaches users Linux basics and the ACFS workflow through hands-on missions.

## Background & Reasoning
Documentation fails beginners. Interactive learning succeeds. The onboard TUI:
- Teaches by doing, not reading
- Builds muscle memory for key commands
- Introduces tools progressively
- Can be re-run anytime as a refresher

## 7 Missions

### Mission 0: Welcome
- What you now have
- The VPS as playground (safe to break, just re-run ACFS)
- Overview of installed tools

### Mission 1: Linux Navigation
- pwd, ls, cd, mkdir, rm
- /home/ubuntu vs /data/projects
- Navigation shortcuts (dev, proj, p aliases)

### Mission 2: SSH & Persistence
- What SSH is
- Why tmux matters (connection drops, tmux lives)
- Basic attach/detach

### Mission 3: tmux Basics
- Create/attach/detach sessions
- Split panes
- Copy mode basics

### Mission 4: Agent Commands
- cc (Claude), cod (Codex), gmi (Gemini)
- First login flow
- `caam backup` right after login

### Mission 5: NTM Command Center
- ntm deps -v (verify)
- ntm tutorial
- ntm spawn demo --cc=2 --cod=1
- ntm send/broadcast

### Mission 6: NTM Command Palette
- Show vendored command_palette.md
- Searchable prompt list
- "Send to all agents" feature

### Mission 7: The Flywheel Loop
- Plan in Beads → bv
- Coordinate via Agent Mail
- Run agents via NTM
- Search via cass
- Build memory via cm
- Scan with ubs
- Optional guardrails via slb

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 7 missions implemented
- [ ] Can be re-run safely anytime
- [ ] Works offline (except optional updates)
- [ ] Takes < 30 minutes to complete all
- [ ] User feels confident to start coding

## Technical Approach
- Shell script or simple Go/Rust binary
- Uses gum or similar for TUI elements
- Stores progress in ~/.acfs/onboard_progress.json","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:25.187518-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:25.187518-05:00","labels":["epic","onboard"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2bn.1","title":"Create onboard TUI shell script","description":"# Task: Create Onboard TUI Shell Script

## Description
Build the interactive onboarding TUI that teaches users the ACFS workflow.

## Location
Install to: `~/.local/bin/onboard`

## UX Flow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│           Welcome to ACFS Onboarding                │
│                                                     │
│  Choose a lesson:                                   │
│                                                     │
│  [1] Welcome & Overview                ✓ completed  │
│  [2] Linux Navigation (5 min)          ● in progress│
│  [3] SSH & Persistence                              │
│  [4] tmux Basics                                    │
│  [5] Agent Commands (cc, cod, gmi)                  │
│  [6] NTM Command Center                             │
│  [7] NTM Prompt Palette                             │
│  [8] The Flywheel Loop                              │
│                                                     │
│  [q] Quit  [r] Restart from beginning               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Implementation Options

### Option A: Pure Bash + gum
Use charmbracelet/gum for TUI elements:
```bash
gum choose "Welcome" "Linux Navigation" "SSH & Persistence" ...
```

### Option B: Simple menu
Plain bash with read:
```bash
while true; do
    clear
    echo "ACFS Onboarding"
    echo "1) Welcome"
    echo "2) Linux Navigation"
    ...
    read -p "Choose: " choice
done
```

## Lesson Format
Each lesson:
1. Brief explanation
2. Command to try
3. Expected output
4. Checkpoint/quiz

## Progress Tracking
Store in ~/.acfs/onboard_progress.json:
```json
{
  "completed": [1, 2, 3],
  "current": 4,
  "started_at": "2025-12-19T..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 8 lessons implemented
- [ ] Progress saved between runs
- [ ] Can be re-run anytime
- [ ] Works over SSH
- [ ] Clear and beginner-friendly","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:56.967996-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:56.967996-05:00","labels":["onboard","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2bn.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2bn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:56.969656-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2du","title":"Set up bun workspaces for monorepo","description":"# Task: Set up Bun Workspaces

## Description
Configure bun workspaces in the root package.json to manage:
- apps/web (Next.js wizard website)
- packages/manifest (manifest utilities)
- packages/onboard (onboard TUI)

## Technical Details
Root package.json should include:
```json
{
  "workspaces": ["apps/*", "packages/*"]
}
```

Each workspace needs its own package.json with appropriate name/scripts.

## Reasoning
Bun workspaces allow:
- Shared dependencies across packages
- Single lockfile (bun.lock)
- Easy cross-package imports
- Consistent tooling

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Root package.json with workspaces config
- [ ] Each workspace has valid package.json
- [ ] `bun install` works from root
- [ ] Cross-package imports resolve correctly

## Dependencies
Depends on directory structure being created first.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:20:33.61383-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:27:33.126119-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:27:33.126119-05:00","close_reason":"Root bun workspace set up with apps/* and packages/* workspaces","labels":["infrastructure","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2du","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-e88","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:20:33.616805-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","title":"EPIC: VPS Installer (install.sh)","description":"# Epic: VPS Installer (install.sh)

## Overview
Build the one-liner bash installer that transforms a fresh Ubuntu VPS into a fully-armed agentic coding environment.

## Background & Reasoning
This is the "magic moment" - the user pastes one command and everything happens. The installer must:
- Work when run as root (common VPS default)
- Work when run as ubuntu with sudo
- Be idempotent (safe to run twice)
- Handle connection drops and resume
- Log everything for debugging

## Design Philosophy
**Vibe Mode Default**: Passwordless sudo, agents with full permissions, minimal friction.
**Checkpointed Phases**: Each phase writes state so reruns skip completed work.
**Module Manifest**: Single source of truth (acfs.manifest.yaml) drives all installs.

## Installer Phases
1. Preflight + confirm OS (Ubuntu 24.04+ / 25.x)
2. Create/normalize `ubuntu` user + sudoers
3. Base packages (apt)
4. Shell (zsh + oh-my-zsh + p10k + plugins)
5. Dev toolchain (bun, uv, rust, go, tmux, rg, ast-grep)
6. Agent stack (claude/codex/gemini CLIs + aliases)
7. Flywheel stack (ntm, slb, ubs, cass, cm, bv, caam, mcp_agent_mail)
8. Post-install UX (onboard + doctor + summary)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Fresh Ubuntu 25.x VPS → working environment in < 15 minutes
- [ ] Safe to run twice without breaking anything
- [ ] All 8 Dicklesworthstone tools installed and verified
- [ ] `acfs doctor` shows all green
- [ ] Logs available at /var/log/acfs/install.log

## Considerations
- Always prefer upstream installers when available
- Verify each tool after install
- Provide clear error messages with fix suggestions
- Support `--dry-run` for transparency","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:06.097272-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:06.097272-05:00","labels":["epic","installer"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.1","title":"Create installer logging library (scripts/lib/logging.sh)","description":"# Task: Create Installer Logging Library

## Description
Build a reusable logging library for consistent installer output.

## Functions

```bash
log_step()    # [1/8] Step description (blue)
log_detail()  # Indented detail (gray)
log_success() # ✔ Success message (green)
log_warn()    # ⚠️ Warning message (yellow)
log_error()   # ✖ Error message (red)
log_debug()   # Only if ACFS_DEBUG=1
```

## Requirements
- All output to stderr (keep stdout clean)
- Support --quiet flag (suppress non-errors)
- Support --verbose flag (show debug)
- Timestamps in log file
- ANSI color codes with fallback

## Log File
Write all output to:
- `/var/log/acfs/install.log`
- `~/.acfs/logs/install.<timestamp>.log`

## Implementation

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# scripts/lib/logging.sh

ACFS_LOG_FILE="/var/log/acfs/install.log"
ACFS_QUIET="${ACFS_QUIET:-0}"
ACFS_VERBOSE="${ACFS_VERBOSE:-0}"

log_step() {
    local step="$1"
    local msg="$2"
    echo -e "\033[34m[$step] $msg\033[0m" >&2
    echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] STEP: $msg" >> "$ACFS_LOG_FILE"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All log functions implemented
- [ ] Colors work in terminal
- [ ] --quiet suppresses output
- [ ] --verbose shows debug
- [ ] Log file written with timestamps
- [ ] Works when sourced by other scripts","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:17.871116-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.34494-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.34494-05:00","close_reason":"Files created in previous session: scripts/lib/logging.sh, os_detect.sh, user.sh, zsh.sh, and install.sh with all 8 phases","labels":["installer","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:17.872789-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

### Via apt (Ubuntu 24.04+)

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.11","title":"Create cloud and database tools installer (scripts/lib/cloud_db.sh)","description":"# Task: Create Cloud & Database Tools Installer

## Description
Install PostgreSQL, HashiCorp Vault, and cloud CLIs.

## Tools to Install

### PostgreSQL 18
```bash
# Add PGDG repo
sudo sh -c 'echo "deb http://apt.postgresql.org/pub/repos/apt $(lsb_release -cs)-pgdg main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/pgdg.list'
curl -fsSL https://www.postgresql.org/media/keys/ACCC4CF8.asc | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/trusted.gpg.d/postgresql.gpg
sudo apt update
sudo apt install -y postgresql-18
```

### HashiCorp Vault
```bash
# Via official HashiCorp repo
```

### Cloud CLIs (via bun)
```bash
bun install -g wrangler      # Cloudflare
bun install -g supabase      # Supabase
bun install -g vercel        # Vercel
```

## Functions
```bash
install_postgresql()
install_vault()
install_cloud_clis()
verify_cloud_db()
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PostgreSQL 18 installed and running
- [ ] Vault CLI available
- [ ] All cloud CLIs installed
- [ ] Verification passes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:54.761033-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:54.761033-05:00","labels":["installer","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.11","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:54.761485-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.12","title":"Create post-install smoke test","description":"# Task: Create Post-Install Smoke Test

## Description
Automatic verification that runs at the end of install.sh.

## What It Tests

### Critical Path (must pass)
1. User is ubuntu
2. Shell is zsh
3. Passwordless sudo works
4. /data/projects exists
5. bun, uv, cargo, go available
6. claude, codex, gemini commands exist
7. ntm command works
8. onboard command exists

### Non-Critical (warn only)
- Agent Mail server can start
- All 8 stack tools respond to --help
- PostgreSQL service running

## Output
```
[Smoke Test]
✅ User: ubuntu
✅ Shell: zsh
✅ Sudo: passwordless
✅ Workspace: /data/projects exists
✅ Languages: bun, uv, cargo, go
✅ Agents: claude, codex, gemini
✅ NTM: working
✅ Onboard: installed

⚠️ Agent Mail: not started (run 'am' to start)
⚠️ PostgreSQL: not running (optional)

Smoke test: 8/8 critical passed, 2 warnings
```

## Integration
- Called automatically at end of install.sh
- Exit 0 if all critical pass
- Exit 1 if any critical fail (with guidance)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All critical checks implemented
- [ ] Clear pass/fail output
- [ ] Warnings for non-critical
- [ ] Runs in < 10 seconds","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:38:39.577781-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:38:39.577781-05:00","labels":["installer","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.12","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:38:39.579953-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

## Description
Automatic verification that runs at the end of install.sh.

## What It Tests

### Critical Path (must pass)
1. User is ubuntu
2. Shell is zsh
3. Passwordless sudo works
4. /data/projects exists
5. bun, uv, cargo, go available
6. claude, codex, gemini commands exist
7. ntm command works
8. onboard command exists

### Non-Critical (warn only)
- Agent Mail server can start
- All 8 stack tools respond to --help
- PostgreSQL service running

## Output
```
[Smoke Test]
✅ User: ubuntu
✅ Shell: zsh
✅ Sudo: passwordless
✅ Workspace: /data/projects exists
✅ Languages: bun, uv, cargo, go
✅ Agents: claude, codex, gemini
✅ NTM: working
✅ Onboard: installed

⚠️ Agent Mail: not started (run 'am' to start)

```

## Integration
- Called automatically at end of install.sh
- Exit 0 if all critical pass
- Exit 1 if any critical fail (with guidance)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All critical checks implemented

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.2","title":"Create OS detection library (scripts/lib/os_detect.sh)","description":"# Task: Create OS Detection Library

## Description
Build a library to detect and validate the target OS.

## Requirements
- Detect Ubuntu version (24.04, 25.x)
- Detect architecture (amd64, arm64)
- Fail gracefully on unsupported OS
- Provide useful error messages

## Functions

```bash
detect_os()          # Returns: ubuntu
detect_version()     # Returns: 25.04
detect_codename()    # Returns: plucky
detect_arch()        # Returns: amd64 or arm64
validate_os()        # Exit 1 if unsupported
```

## Supported Configurations
- Ubuntu 24.04 LTS (noble)
- Ubuntu 25.04 (plucky)
- Ubuntu 25.10 (future)
- Architectures: amd64, arm64

## Implementation

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# scripts/lib/os_detect.sh

detect_os() {
    if [[ -f /etc/os-release ]]; then
        . /etc/os-release
        echo "$ID"
    else
        echo "unknown"
    fi
}

validate_os() {
    local os=$(detect_os)
    local version=$(detect_version)
    
    if [[ "$os" != "ubuntu" ]]; then
        log_error "ACFS requires Ubuntu. Detected: $os"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    # Check version >= 24.04
    if [[ "${version%.*}" -lt 24 ]]; then
        log_error "ACFS requires Ubuntu 24.04 or later. Detected: $version"
        exit 1
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Correctly detects Ubuntu versions
- [ ] Detects arm64 vs amd64
- [ ] Clear error on unsupported OS
- [ ] Works on fresh Ubuntu VPS","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:30.433099-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.346095-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.346095-05:00","close_reason":"Files created in previous session: scripts/lib/logging.sh, os_detect.sh, user.sh, zsh.sh, and install.sh with all 8 phases","labels":["installer","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:30.435198-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.3","title":"Create user normalization library (scripts/lib/user.sh)","description":"# Task: Create User Normalization Library

## Description
Build a library to ensure the ubuntu user exists with correct configuration.

## Requirements
- Create ubuntu user if missing
- Set up passwordless sudo
- Copy SSH authorized_keys from root
- Set zsh as default shell (after zsh installed)

## Functions

```bash
ensure_ubuntu_user()     # Create user if missing
setup_passwordless_sudo() # Write sudoers.d file
copy_ssh_keys()          # From root to ubuntu
set_default_shell()      # Change to zsh
```

## Sudoers Configuration
File: `/etc/sudoers.d/90-ubuntu-acfs`
```
ubuntu ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL
```

## SSH Key Handling
If running as root:
1. Check /root/.ssh/authorized_keys exists
2. Create /home/ubuntu/.ssh/ if needed
3. Copy authorized_keys
4. Fix permissions (700, 600)
5. Set ownership to ubuntu:ubuntu

## Groups
Add ubuntu to:
- sudo
- docker (after docker installed)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Creates ubuntu user if missing
- [ ] Passwordless sudo works
- [ ] SSH keys copied from root
- [ ] Correct file permissions
- [ ] Works when run as root or ubuntu
- [ ] Idempotent (safe to run twice)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:42.426684-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.346616-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.346616-05:00","close_reason":"Files created in previous session: scripts/lib/logging.sh, os_detect.sh, user.sh, zsh.sh, and install.sh with all 8 phases","labels":["installer","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.3","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:42.428514-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.4","title":"Create shell setup library (scripts/lib/zsh.sh)","description":"# Task: Create Shell Setup Library

## Description
Build a library to install and configure the complete shell environment.

## Components
1. zsh installation
2. Oh My Zsh installation
3. Powerlevel10k theme
4. Plugins installation
5. ACFS zshrc deployment

## Functions

```bash
install_zsh()              # apt install zsh
install_oh_my_zsh()        # Official installer
install_powerlevel10k()    # Clone to custom themes
install_zsh_plugins()      # Clone plugins
deploy_acfs_zshrc()        # Copy acfs.zshrc
configure_zshrc()          # Set up loader
```

## Plugins to Install
- zsh-autosuggestions
- zsh-syntax-highlighting
- docker
- docker-compose
- sudo
- colored-man-pages
- command-not-found
- git (built-in)

## File Layout
```
~/.acfs/
├── zsh/
│   └── acfs.zshrc     # Main config
└── ...

~/.zshrc                # Tiny loader
~/.zshrc.local          # User overrides (touch)
~/.p10k.zsh             # Theme config
```

## zshrc Loader Content
```bash
# ACFS loader
source "$HOME/.acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc"
[ -f "$HOME/.zshrc.local" ] && source "$HOME/.zshrc.local"
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] zsh installed and working
- [ ] Oh My Zsh installed
- [ ] Powerlevel10k theme active
- [ ] All plugins installed
- [ ] acfs.zshrc deployed
- [ ] Loader .zshrc in place
- [ ] User overrides supported","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:55.348206-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.346999-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.346999-05:00","close_reason":"Files created in previous session: scripts/lib/logging.sh, os_detect.sh, user.sh, zsh.sh, and install.sh with all 8 phases","labels":["installer","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.4","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:55.350105-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kwk","title":"Vendor NTM command_palette.md for onboarding","description":"# Task: Vendor NTM Command Palette

## Description
Copy and maintain a local version of NTM's command_palette.md for use in onboarding.

## Background
The onboard TUI needs to show NTM's prompt palette to users. Rather than fetch it live (requires internet), we vendor a copy.

## Implementation

### Vendor Location
`acfs/onboard/docs/ntm/command_palette.md`

### Sync Script
`scripts/sync/sync_ntm_palette.sh`

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Sync NTM command palette from upstream

SOURCE="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/main/command_palette.md"
DEST="acfs/onboard/docs/ntm/command_palette.md"

curl -fsSL "$SOURCE" > "$DEST"
echo "Synced command_palette.md"
```

### Install Location
Deployed to: `~/.acfs/docs/ntm/command_palette.md`

### Onboard Integration
In Mission 6, show the palette using:
- bat (pretty) if available
- less as fallback

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sync script works
- [ ] Palette included in installer
- [ ] Displayed in onboard Mission 6
- [ ] Readable over SSH","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:28:15.931606-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:28:15.931606-05:00","labels":["onboard","task"]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-tof","title":"Create README.md for the project","description":"# Task: Create README.md

For users:
```bash
# Visit the wizard
https://acfs.dev

```

### What Gets Installed
- Shell environment (zsh, oh-my-zsh, powerlevel10k)
- Languages (bun, uv, rust, go)
- Coding agents (Claude, Codex, Gemini)
- The Dicklesworthstone stack (8 tools)

### Screenshots/GIFs
- Wizard interface
- Terminal after install
- ntm in action

### Development
For contributors:
```bash
git clone ...
cd apps/web
bun install
bun run dev
```

### Architecture
Brief overview of:
- apps/web (website)
- install.sh (installer)
- acfs/ (configs)
- packages/ (shared code)

## Do NOT Include
- Contributing section (per AGENTS.md)

- Unnecessary sections

- [ ] Quick start for end users
- [ ] Development instructions

-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vqr","title":"Create SSH troubleshooting guide","description":"# Task: Create SSH Troubleshooting Guide

## Description
Comprehensive guide for common SSH connection failures.

## Location
- Website: Embedded in wizard step 6
- Installer: ~/.acfs/docs/ssh_troubleshooting.md

## Common Issues Covered

### Permission denied (publickey)
**Symptoms:** `Permission denied (publickey)`
**Causes:**
1. Wrong key file specified
2. Key not added to VPS
3. Wrong username

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Verify key exists
ls -la ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519*

# Try with verbose output
ssh -v -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@IP
```

### Connection refused
**Symptoms:** `Connection refused`
**Causes:**
1. SSH service not running
2. Firewall blocking port 22
3. Wrong IP address

**Fixes:**
- Check IP is correct
- VPS provider may need firewall rule

### Connection timeout
**Symptoms:** `Connection timed out`
**Causes:**
1. Wrong IP
2. VPS not fully booted
3. Network issue

**Fixes:**
- Wait 2-3 minutes for VPS to boot
- Verify IP in provider dashboard

### Host key verification failed
**Symptoms:** `Host key verification failed`
**Causes:** 
- VPS was recreated with same IP
- Man-in-the-middle warning

**Fixes:**
```bash
ssh-keygen -R IP_ADDRESS
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All common issues documented
- [ ] Clear cause → fix structure
- [ ] Commands are copy-paste ready
- [ ] Integrated in website step 6","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:39:03.569312-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:39:03.569312-05:00","labels":["documentation","task"]}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","title":"EPIC: Website Wizard MVP","description":"# Epic: Website Wizard MVP

## Overview
Build the step-by-step wizard website that guides beginners from "I have a laptop" to "paste the curl|bash command".

## Background & Reasoning
The wizard is the user's first touchpoint with ACFS. It must:
- Feel premium and trustworthy
- Never overwhelm with jargon
- Make every step feel achievable
- Handle both Mac and Windows users
- Guide through VPS provider selection

## Tech Stack
- Next.js 16 App Router
- Bun runtime
- Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui
- Vercel hosting + Cloudflare caching

## Key Design Principles
1. **Single action per screen** - One button or one command
2. **Copy button on every command** - Reduce friction
3. **"I did it" checkbox** - Gates "Next" button
4. **Collapsible troubleshooting** - Don't clutter but be ready

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 10 wizard steps implemented
- [ ] OS-aware command rendering
- [ ] Mobile responsive
- [ ] Lighthouse score > 90
- [ ] Works without JavaScript (progressive enhancement)

## Relationship to Project Goals
The wizard is the "front door" to ACFS. A confused or frustrated user at this stage will never reach the VPS installer. Excellence here directly enables the 10-minute promise.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:20:46.959703-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:20:46.959703-05:00","labels":["epic","website"]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.1","title":"Initialize Next.js 16 App Router project","description":"# Task: Initialize Next.js 16 App Router Project

## Description
Set up the Next.js 16 project in apps/web/ with:
- App Router structure
- TypeScript configuration
- Tailwind CSS
- shadcn/ui components

## Technical Details
```bash
cd apps/web
bunx create-next-app@latest . --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir
bunx shadcn-ui@latest init
```

## Configuration
- Use Bun as package manager
- Configure for Vercel deployment
- Set up path aliases (@/components, @/lib)
- Configure next.config.js for optimal settings

## Reasoning
Next.js 16 with App Router provides:
- Modern React Server Components
- Automatic code splitting
- Optimal performance defaults
- Easy Vercel deployment

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Next.js 16 project created
- [ ] TypeScript strict mode enabled
- [ ] Tailwind CSS working
- [ ] shadcn/ui initialized
- [ ] bun run dev works
- [ ] bun run build succeeds","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:22:18.88745-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:27:32.977854-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:27:32.977854-05:00","close_reason":"Next.js 16 project initialized with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn/ui, and turbopack. ## Description
Help users make their first SSH connection to the VPS.

## Why This Is Critical
This is where most beginners fail:
- Wrong username (root vs ubuntu)
- Key permissions wrong
- Firewall blocking port 22
- Host key verification confusion

## Key Elements
- Command using stored IP from step 5
- OS-aware command (Mac vs Windows)
- "Try ubuntu@, if fails try root@" guidance
- Host key verification explanation
- Comprehensive troubleshooting section

## Troubleshooting Covered
- Permission denied (publickey)
- Connection refused
- Connection timeout
- Host key verification failed

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] OS-specific variants shown
- [ ] ubuntu/root fallback explained
- [ ] Troubleshooting expandable section","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:07.524339-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:55:35.709759-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:55:35.709759-05:00","close_reason":"Closed","labels":["mvp","task","website","wizard-step"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.11","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:07.526273-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
